ugf_response: can be liked more than the best one. we can think about this by looking at how much time, work and things we have to spend. if we need something now, a quick answer that works well for what we need is better than a perfect answer that takes too long to find. if we have only a little of something, the simple answer is often the right one. if we do not know much, using a simple answer lets us keep moving and still get what we want. if we wait for the perfect answer, we might miss the chance to use anything at all. also, when many people are in a hurry or things may get hurt, a clear perfect answer is probably better because it is spread out and can cause big trouble. so a good enough one is an idea where the thing we must give up to look for the very best is bigger than the small thing we will lose from not having something. when the chance of bad is low, when the question is simple, and when waiting would make other problems worse, it is a good pick to take a quick, easy answer. when the problem may change or might become even worse later, it is less able to to take the fast, clear choice. in those cases we should try for the best answer no matter what: especially when we have enough time, money, and the chance of trouble.
